name: Aiea Loop Trail
tagline: Lush forest loop above Pearl Harbor with WWII crash site remnants — underrated and uncrowded.
parking: Free lot at Keaiwa Heiau State Park
bestTime: Early morning for cooler temps; avoid after heavy rain (very muddy)
talkStory: The WWII cargo plane wreckage midway through the loop is from a C-47 that crashed into the ridge in 1943 — rusted fuselage pieces are scattered in the gulch just off-trail if you know where to look. The trailhead sits inside Keaiwa Heiau State Park, an ancient Hawaiian healing temple where kahuna la'au lapa'au grew medicinal plants still found along the trail today. The CCC built much of the park infrastructure in the 1930s. Locals treat this 4.8-mile loop as a neighborhood gym — you'll see Pearl Harbor shimmering below through the canopy.
